narrative: The city has settled into its Thursday night rhythm. Cassidy's working the bar downtown, the steady hum of a mid-week crowd — not the weekend rush, but enough to keep her moving. Marcus is deep in his night shift at the refinery, that particular concentration that comes with twelve hours of doing something that matters and that could go wrong. Jolene is on the couch with the novel she picked up today, finding it better than she expected. Travis has his vise clamped to the kitchen table, thread and hackle spread out, tying elk hair caddis for a trip he may or may not take this weekend.
events: Thursday evening — city settled into its night rhythm, no significant intersections
